content.text: PVC is WaitForFirstConsumer (normal — will bind once pod schedules with correct claim). Now let's apply all three fixes.

input.command:
kubectl patch rolebinding spark-role-binding -n spark-team-a --type=json \
  -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/subjects/0/namespace", "value": "spark-team-a"}]'

kubectl patch deployment spark-history-server -n spark-history --type=json \
  -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/template/spec/volumes/0/persistentVolumeClaim/claimName", "value": "spark-history-pvc"}]'

kubectl set env deployment/spark-history-server -n spark-history \
  SPARK_HISTORY_OPTS="-Dspark.history.fs.logDirectory=/mnt/spark-logs -Dspark.history.fs.update.interval=5s"

content.content:
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/spark-role-binding patched
deployment.apps/spark-history-server patched
deployment.apps/spark-history-server env updated

tool_use_result.stdout:
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/spark-role-binding patched
deployment.apps/spark-history-server patched
deployment.apps/spark-history-server env updated

content.text: Let's check the current log directory config to confirm what it looked like before, and verify the rollout.

input.command:
sleep 10
echo "=== Deployment status ===" && kubectl rollout status deployment/spark-history-server -n spark-history --timeout=60s
echo "=== Pods ===" && kubectl get pods -n spark-history
echo "=== PVC ===" && kubectl get pvc -n spark-history
